title: The Iron Blockade
summary: The crew needs a fleet. The Leviathan is heading to Malpaz for a demonstration strike, and the people who rose up because of the crew's actions in Adventure 5 are the target. Hunted and battered, the crew arrives at Black Spire Outpost on Batuu — the one place in the galaxy where the Empire's reach is short and the local boss has the firepower to help. Oga Garra is transactional, not ideological. She'll assemble a pirate fleet because Leviathan is bad for business, but she'll collect on the debt.

gearNotes.reason: Negotiating fleet terms while Imperial hunters are in-system. Intercepted communications here could compromise the entire alliance.
The crew arrives battered from the Crucible. Patch up before the blockade run.
The blockade run requires ship repairs under fire. Oga's fleet is held together with spit and prayer.
Bypassing Imperial blockade sensors and communications during the breakout.
Imperial interdiction forces during the blockade run. Disabling systems buys time.
Extended ship combat and boarding actions during the blockade run. No downtime.
Black Spire Outpost is a smuggler's haven, but Imperial spies operate there. Keeping a low profile while negotiating with Oga.

scenes.title: Assembling the Fleet
scenes.subtitle: Deals and Debts
scenes.gmNotes: SOREN VEX REVEAL: Among the captains Oga puts forward is Soren Vex — Maya's former partner who betrayed her during a refugee smuggling run and turned her in to the Empire for a bounty. He operates a battered Corellian gunship called the Requital out of Black Spire Outpost. Play the reveal cold: no buildup, no foreshadowing. Soren is just there when the captains assemble. Maya recognizes him instantly. The room goes quiet. Soren doesn't run and doesn't apologize. He cooperates professionally. If the heroes press him for the story, he gives it in flat, factual terms — no self-pity, no justification. He turned her in. The Imperials took the refugees. He took the bounty. He left. Maya can confirm or deny. Let the tension simmer through the rest of the fleet assembly. Soren is competent and his ship is needed — the question is whether Maya (and the crew) can work with the man who destroyed her life. This is not a scene that needs resolution here. The resolution comes at Malpaz. IF MAYA DIED IN ADV3: The reveal shifts — the crew learns about their dead pilot's past from the man she'll never confront. Let a crew member discover who Soren is through Switch intel, Oga's briefing, or Soren's own drunken confession. The emotional weight becomes retrospective grief rather than live confrontation.

npcs.name: Soren Vex
npcs.notes: Maya's former partner. Turned her in to the Empire during a refugee run. Now a burned-out smuggler captain in Oga's orbit. Flies the Requital. Competent, tired, quietly ashamed. Does not apologize unless cornered — and even then, he knows words aren't enough.

threatBuild.name: Soren Vex
threatBuild.notes: Not a combat encounter in Adv8. Stats provided for Adv10 fleet battle and any contingency where the heroes confront him physically.
